*📚 GO DEEPER INTO ETHIOPIA*

*🍲 BRING ETHIOPIA HOME*

comments.text: Please Ethiopia remain together. Settle your differences. Let Ethiopia show the rest of Africa that TOGETHER we stand.  In my youth Addis Ababa was as sophisticated  as London or Paris. So hold together and get your dignity back. The rest of Africa needs you to. Like Iraq and Lydia, outsiders are waiting to loot your heritage. So remain together. May you have just and fair leaders and May God bless Ethiopia. Amen.

comments.author: @PictorialJourney
comments.text: The way you filmed those quiet, hidden corners of the landscape really captured a sense of stillness that is hard to find these days. I love how the camera just moves at its own steady pace, letting you soak in the natural beauty without any forced editing or loud transitions. You can tell a lot of patience went into getting these shots just right, and the choice of music fits the vibe perfectly. It feels like such a gentle, authentic invitation to breathe and reset for a few minutes. Thank you for putting this together and helping me find a bit of calm today. 🍃🎶💚

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: That's the quietest kind of compliment — thank you for letting it land. More journeys ahead. 🌿

comments.author: @muhammadal-hashimi2487
comments.text: This video is a gross distortion! First of all, Ethiopia is an empire that forced several independent polities into a construct called Ethiopia. The Amhara army of aggression under the leadership of the King of Showa, who would later become crowned Emperor Menelik, would by force of arms, by naked imperial aggression, force many former independent kingdons and sultanates into a colonial contruct under his control. Menelik II was as much an aggressor against independent peoples as was any European imperial aggressor during the Scramble for Africa! This is one of the main reasons why there is so much ethnic tension in Ethiopia today. The majority of its people were forced into a union called Ethiopia looking down the barrel of a gun. And lets not forget that the Ethiopian empire has already began to break up when the Ethiopian province of Eritrea fought for and won its independence. Tigray might be next to break away! And Oromia may not be too far around the the corner  to re-establish the sovereignty it enjoyed before the naked imperial agreession of Menelik II!

comments.author: @alemtsegay8595
comments.text: What a bigotry!!! Italians were not defeated in Adawa only but also in Dogali and others.  They tried several times, suffice,  defeated miserably at all battle field. Adawa was the culmination, the mother of all battle. Nevertheless, the battle belongs to Abyssenia (Habesha) the  gallant Axumit  that's the Tigrawot fighters. King Menilik's army and queen tsehtu's army  never crossed Emba Alage. There are a lot of information buried underneath,  you need to delve into genuine historical searches and only then maybe qualified your narration as historical chronicles. Otherwise,  the Ethiopia which was established for geopolitics of  western imperialistic colonialism has stolen the Abyssenian(Habesha-Axum) history, linguistic and heritage as if it belongs to modern Ethiopia. What a bravado  geopolitical hallucinations !!! Enough is  enough, let historical chronicles narrated properly to factual hygienic events! Thanks!!!

comments.author: @CanuckBeaver
comments.text: A very good AI narration, hitting the high points and respectful. In western Canada, I drink only Ethiopian coffee from a small importer. In the good old days... like up to last year.... documentaries were expensive, with trips over years, teamwork, photography to support the story. -- Now a single person can ask AI to write a story and then the pictures are  just stock footage, which leaves big mismatches between the text and the images.  At 84, I regret the massive loss of quality and enthusiasm. I guess about 95% of YouTube documentaries on every topic -- news commentary, war stories, science, etc, have a droning voice and mismatch of pictures. I would rate this maybe a B, very high for AI documentaries which are mostly garbage at the D or F level, which I usually never finish.   Until the producers go to the tiny extra effort to match all images to the narration, even stock images, which can be still shots,  AI slop remains AI slop. But this is good enough for me, a retired science teacher, to subscribe for a while because I learned a lot and stuck with it, only groaning at the laziness to not edit the pictures better.
